skill.name: fixing-metadata
skill.description: Review and improve page metadata for titles, descriptions, canonical URLs, social cards, icons, manifests, structured data, and locale alternates.
skill.summary: Helps teams ship complete, consistent page metadata with minimal code changes.
skill.author: ibelick

security_audit.summary: The static findings were reviewed against the single skill file and are false positives. Markdown examples of slash-command usage were misread as Ruby backticks, and metadata priority text was misclassified as cryptography or reconnaissance. No prompt injection, malicious intent, code execution, network access, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Command Examples
low_findings.description: Lines 12 and 15 show slash-command examples inside Markdown backticks. They are documentation text, not Ruby shell execution or executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding section is usage documentation, and the backticked text is a slash command name. No script body, shell invocation, or user input execution path exists.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Metadata Text Classified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The referenced lines describe SEO metadata rules, priority labels, titles, descriptions, and social cards. No weak cryptographic algorithm or cryptographic operation appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found ordinary metadata guidance on every referenced line. There is no hash function, cipher, certificate handling, or security-sensitive crypto context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Metadata Guidance Classified as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The referenced lines discuss metadata systems, title stuffing, manifest validity, theme color, and JSON-LD validity. They do not gather host or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain static review guidance only. No commands, filesystem probes, environment reads, process listing, or network discovery behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Incomplete metadata causes duplicate tags, poor previews, and incorrect indexing. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused checklist for fixing metadata safely.
actual_capabilities: Checks titles, descriptions, canonical URLs, robots tags, and duplicate metadata definitions.
Reviews Open Graph and Twitter card fields for consistency with canonical URLs.
Guides favicon, app icon, manifest, and theme-color implementation decisions.
Evaluates JSON-LD only when it matches visible page content.
Reviews locale, hreflang, and canonical behavior for localized pages.
Encourages minimal code changes that follow the existing metadata pattern.
limitations: Does not include executable validators or automated browser checks.
Does not replace search engine documentation or platform-specific requirements.
Requires project files or snippets to identify concrete metadata issues.
Does not add new SEO libraries unless the user explicitly requests them.

use_cases.title: Pre-release metadata review
use_cases.description: Audit a new page before launch and identify missing titles, descriptions, canonical URLs, and social preview fields.

use_cases.title: SEO consistency cleanup
use_cases.description: Find duplicated or conflicting metadata definitions across layouts, pages, and shared metadata helpers.

use_cases.title: Localized page metadata check
use_cases.description: Review language tags, hreflang alternates, locale-specific canonical URLs, and localized social metadata.

prompt_templates.title: Review one page
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single page file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use fixing-metadata to review this page file. List metadata problems, why each matters, and the smallest safe fix.

prompt_templates.title: Check social sharing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use fixing-metadata to check Open Graph and Twitter card metadata for this page. Verify titles, descriptions, images, and canonical alignment.

prompt_templates.title: Audit duplicate metadata
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review across shared layout and page files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use fixing-metadata to compare these metadata definitions. Find duplicate or competing tags and suggest one consistent source of truth.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a metadata refactor
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before changing a shared metadata system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use fixing-metadata to create a minimal migration plan for this metadata system. Preserve existing behavior and identify release risks.

output_examples.input: A product page has a title, but no description or social image.
output: Missing meta description reduces search and sharing clarity.
Missing social image can produce weak link previews.
Add a concise description and an absolute image URL.

output_examples.input: A localized page uses one canonical URL for every language.
output: The canonical URL does not match each locale.
Search engines may consolidate localized pages incorrectly.
Use locale-specific canonicals and only add alternates for existing pages.

output_examples.input: A site has metadata in both a layout helper and each page component.
output: Competing metadata systems can emit duplicate tags.
Choose one source of truth per page.
Keep the fix scoped to metadata definitions.

best_practices: Provide the exact page, layout, or metadata helper file before asking for fixes.
Verify canonical URLs, social images, and previews on a real deployed URL.
Keep metadata changes small and aligned with the existing framework pattern.
anti_patterns: Do not add structured data for content that is not visible on the page.
Do not introduce a new SEO library for a small metadata correction.
Do not use random, unstable, or user-generated strings without sanitization.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews page metadata for correctness, duplication, search indexing, social previews, icons, manifests, structured data, and localization.

faq.question: Can it edit my code directly?
faq.answer: It can guide code-level changes when used in a coding agent, but the skill itself is written guidance.

faq.question: Does it validate live search results?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on implementation review and recommends checking live previews on deployed URLs.

faq.question: Which frameworks does it support?
faq.answer: It can apply to Next.js metadata, React Helmet, manual head tags, and similar project-specific metadata patterns.

faq.question: Does it generate structured data?
faq.answer: It can suggest structured data only when it clearly matches real visible page content.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains metadata guidance only, with no executable code or malicious instructions.
